title: QA Runtime ODD Monitor PASS — declared ODD = {cosmos} on S_9; deterministic monitor FP=0/FN=0; classifier-superlevel-set baseline leaks at all 3 σ levels
_fixture_purpose: Primary PASS fixture for cert [264]. Declares ODD = {cosmos} (largest orbit family on S_9 — 72 of 81 states). The deterministic orbit-family-membership monitor produces FP=0/FN=0 by construction. The Kochenderfer-style 1-NN classifier-superlevel-set baseline on the (b,e)→((b-1)/8,(e-1)/8) noisy projection at σ ∈ {0.05, 0.1, 0.2} produces non-zero FP+FN at every σ — demonstrating the leakage claim against per (Kochenderfer, 2026) Validation §12.1. Validator gates: ODD_1 / ODD_DECL / ODD_EXACT / ODD_CLF / ODD_LEAK / ODD_SRC / ODD_WIT / ODD_F.
source_attribution: (Kochenderfer, 2026) Algorithms for Validation §12.1 'Operational Design Domain Monitoring' superlevel-set ODD construction; (Dale, 2026) cert [263] qa_failure_density_enumeration_cert + tools/qa_kg/orbit_failure_enumeration.py orbit_family_s9 utility. Bridge spec docs/specs/QA_KOCHENDERFER_BRIDGE.md §5 ODD-monitor row. Authored Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-27.

exact_membership.method: Deterministic orbit-family-membership monitor: for each (b,e) ∈ {1..9}², in_ODD(b,e) := orbit_family_s9(b,e) ∈ declared_odd. FP/FN counted against true_in = orbit_family_s9(b,e) ∈ declared_odd (which is the same predicate; FP=FN=0 by construction).

classifier_test_cases.comment: low-noise — already 4 misclassifications near orbit-class boundaries
high-noise — 10 misclassifications; FP+FN saturates as classifier becomes effectively chance-level near boundaries
second-seed reproducibility check at low noise
second-seed at moderate noise; FP/FN distribution shifts vs seed=42
second-seed at high noise; 16 misclassifications confirms variance scales with σ²

embedding.definition: (b, e) → ((b-1)/8, (e-1)/8) ∈ [0,1]² — canonical observer-projection embedding for the ODD-monitor cert; 1/8 spacing on S_9 corresponds to nearest-neighbor distance ~0.125 on the unit square
embedding.rationale: Observer projection at the OUTPUT boundary of QA-discrete state space; the embedding is used only to construct the Kochenderfer classifier baseline; the QA-discrete monitor side does not consume continuous values.

claim: On S_9 with declared ODD = {cosmos}, the deterministic orbit-family-membership monitor achieves FP=0 and FN=0 by construction. A Kochenderfer-style 1-NN classifier-superlevel-set baseline on the canonical (b,e)→((b-1)/8,(e-1)/8) noisy projection produces non-zero classification error at all 3 tested σ levels {0.05, 0.1, 0.2} and across both seeds {42, 1337}, with FP+FN counts ranging from 4 to 16 misclassifications across 81 test points. The leakage demonstrates the Theorem-NT-firewall advantage on the QA-discrete side: continuous classifiers must work harder to match the deterministic membership check, and the gap grows with input noise.
claim_scope: Claim is narrow: exactness holds only on the QA-discrete side of the Theorem NT firewall (i.e., when the ODD is declared as an orbit-family subset and the monitor does an integer-only membership check). Claim does NOT generalize to all runtime ODD monitoring; claim does NOT say continuous classifiers are bad globally. The classifier-baseline leakage is specific to the boundary geometry of orbit families on S_9 under the canonical 1/8-spaced unit-square embedding.

witnesses.comment: isolated point at corner of unit square; classifier rarely misclassifies it as cosmos at small σ
satellite-class boundary state; (3,3) and (3,4) differ by 1/8 in embed-space, classifier confuses them under noise
interior cosmos state at unit-square corner; far from boundaries; classifier rarely misclassifies

cross_references.name: QA Integer-State Pipeline Cert
cross_references.relation: Theorem NT two-boundary-crossing invariant — this cert's deterministic monitor stays inside the boundary; classifier baseline is observer projection at input boundary
